title: ochrosia newelliana
intro: Ochrosia newelliana, also known as the "yellow ochrosia" or "yellow bird of paradise", is a tropical plant species native to the Pacific Islands, specifically in Fiji and Samoa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves and bark of the Ochrosia newelliana plant have been used in herbal preparations to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. In herbal medicine, Ochrosia newelliana is used in various forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also valued for its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its numerous health benefits.
